subsystem_taxonomy.name: Runtime Configuration, Presets, And Durable State
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own startup configuration, environment parsing, knobs, presets, persistent runtime identity, and reproducible launch state.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Does not own trading decisions, broker sessions, market observations, or UI presentation.

subsystem_taxonomy.name: Market Time, Sessions, Bars, And Series Semantics
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own canonical timestamps, calendars, sessions, bar completeness, resampling meaning, and shared series identity.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Does not own cache storage, broker subscriptions, signals, or simulated fills.

subsystem_taxonomy.name: Signals, Regimes, Shocks, And Market-State Intelligence
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own market observation, feature extraction, signal snapshots, regime routing, shock detection, and context requirements.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Produces facts and classifications; it does not choose position intent, size, or order action.

subsystem_taxonomy.name: Policy, Entry/Exit Gates, Risk, And Position Sizing
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own action decisions derived from market facts: hold, enter, exit, resize, risk overlays, limits, and quantities.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Does not ingest broker data, simulate fills, submit orders, or render operator UI.

subsystem_taxonomy.name: Current-Market Realism And Backtest/Live Parity
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own executable receipts proving simulated and live-intended semantics agree under realistic market and broker conditions.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Validates cross-system truth; it does not become the primary owner of the underlying production implementation.

subsystem_taxonomy.name: Broker Connectivity, Sessions, Accounts, Portfolio, And PnL Truth
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own IB Gateway transport/session lifecycle, client IDs, reconnects, managed accounts, portfolio, account values, and broker PnL truth.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Does not own instrument discovery, quote-quality policy, order execution, or UI formatting.

subsystem_taxonomy.name: Live Market Data, Instrument Identity, And Contract Discovery
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own contract qualification/search, exchange identity, subscriptions, quote provenance, freshness, delayed/frozen modes, and tick increments.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Does not own trading decisions, order state machines, account totals, or presentation-only rendering.

subsystem_taxonomy.name: Live Order Construction, Submission, Reconciliation, And Recovery
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own executable orders, pricing, submission, modification, cancellation, reconciliation, fill progression, repricing, and recovery.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Does not own policy intent, raw market-data ingestion, account truth, or display-only UI.

subsystem_taxonomy.name: Operator UI, Diagnostics, Journaling, And Explainability
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own Textual presentation, controls, status surfaces, journal output, notices, layout, and operator-visible causal truth.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Does not own underlying broker, signal, policy, or order semantics merely because it displays them.

subsystem_taxonomy.name: Backtest Data Acquisition, Cache Integrity, Repair, And Resampling
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own historical acquisition, cache keys and provenance, coverage, gap repair, stitching, persistence, and efficient reuse.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Does not own shared calendar meaning, strategy decisions, simulated fills, or optimization search.

subsystem_taxonomy.name: Backtest Execution Simulation, Portfolio Accounting, And Results
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own simulated order/fill progression, trade lifecycle, equity/PnL accounting, summaries, and deterministic replay results.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Does not own source-data caching, signal/policy definitions, or parameter-search methodology.

subsystem_taxonomy.name: Research, Calibration, Scenario Analysis, And Optimization
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own sweeps, fingerprints, parameter search, calibration, scenario comparison, predictive mining, and champion selection.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Does not redefine simulator truth, production policy semantics, or cache correctness.

subsystem_taxonomy.name: Capability Ledgers, Test Ownership, Canaries, Benchmarks, And Evolution Gates
subsystem_taxonomy.purpose: Own the MECC registry, structural validation, evidence ownership, explicit live canaries, benchmark results, and future-capability promotion.
subsystem_taxonomy.boundary: Proves product capabilities but does not absorb their primary subsystem ownership.

contracts.capability: Configuration, preset payloads, and runtime state remain normalized and loadable.
oracle: all evidence assigned to runtime-configuration-state passes
the capability remains within its MECC ownership boundary
contracts.next: Split unit.runtime.configuration-state only when narrower evidence and oracles exist.

contracts.capability: A self-cleaning read-only canary proves q can reach the localhost-trusted Gateway and connect all three TradeBot clients.
oracle: Gateway trust remains exactly localhost-only
the tunneled read-only IB session exposes a valid server version and managed account
TradeBot main, proxy, and index clients connect with three distinct client IDs
the canary removes its SSH tunnel and temporary client-ID state
contracts.next: Evolve tunnel supervision only through the separate future failover contract.

contracts.capability: q reaches the MacBook IB Gateway listener over direct LAN TCP without widening Gateway authorization.
oracle: q opens a TCP connection to the MacBook Gateway listener
the probe sends no IB order, market-data request, or Gateway mutation
contracts.next: Keep the direct transport probe non-mutating and retain the localhost authorization boundary.

contracts.capability: Score behavioural drift between replayed live traces and backtest decisions/fills.
oracle: a measurable pass/fail outcome exists
promotion requires executable evidence
contracts.next: Design the smallest falsifiable TDD birth for benchmark.future.live-backtest-drift-score.
